Protocolo: Antennae from every 10 mosquitoes of the same species and gender were pooled into one biological replicate, and 4 biological replicates were set for each species-gender combination. Total RNA was extracted from antenna samples using the TRIzol method

For each sample, an appropriate number of midguts were dissected from female mosquitoes, then washed and temporarily stored in Schneider's Drosophila Medium (SDM) within a microcentrifuge tube on ice. After dissection, the medium was discarded, and the midguts were finely minced and then 300 μl of dissociation buffer was added, followed by further mincing. The tissue fragments were resuspended in 700 μl of dissociation buffer and then incubated in a 28 °C water bath for 15-25 min. The suspension was mixed and the cell concentration, viability and aggregation were measured by staining with 0.4% trypan blue every five minutes. Once the quality control  requirements were met, the suspension was loaded into a 50 ml centrifuge tube equipped with a pre-wetted 40 μm filter to remove larger cell debris, and then washed with 1× PBS until the final volume reached 15 ml. The resulting filter was centrifuged at 350× g, 4 °C for 5 min. A viable cell band could be found near or at the bottom of the tube and 100-200 μl of the supernatant was retained to avoid disturbing the pellet. The cell pellet was suspended, followed by a second counting with 0.4% trypan blue. Cell suspension samples with viability ≥ 80% and were used for subsequent scRNA-seq. According to the manufacturer's guidelines, the single-cell suspension was adjusted to appropriate concentration prior to loading onto a microfluidic microwell SCOPE-chip. The cDNA synthesis of captured cells and sequencing library construction were performed with GEXSCOPE Single Cell RNA Library Kit.

Animals were homogenized in Trizol reagent. RNA was harvested using Trizol reagent and Direct-Zol RNA extraction kit (Zymo Research). 2.5 ug of total RNA was used for the construction of sequencing libraries. RNA libraries for RNA-seq were prepared using TruSeq Stranded mRNA Library Prep Kit (Illumina) following manufacturer's protocols.

Total RNA was extracted using the Trizol method following the manufucturers instructions 1 μg of total RNA was subjected to library synthesis using the TruSeq RNA Library Preparation Kit (Illumina). Three independent biological replicates of each group were performed, giving three RNA-seq libraries per group. 1 μg of total RNA was enriched for the Poly-A mRNA and reverse transcribed to double-stranded cDNA. The cDNA was end repaired, adenylated, and ligated with Illumina indexes prior to 12 cycles of PCR.

Extract protocol used was developed for fatty tissues using QIAzol Lysis Reagent. RNA products were purified with the QIAGEN® RNeasy Mini Kit. RNA libraries for RNA-seq were prepared using PerkinElmer NEXTFLEX® Rapid Directional RNA-Seq Kit 2.0 following manufacturer's protocols.

Total RNA from cell pellets and tissues were extracted with the Tri-reagent protocol of Chomzcynski and and Sacchi, 1987. New Englad Bioloabs, NEBNext small RNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina. 8 to 20 libraries with different barcodes were multiplexed in each sequencing run.  Separation of sequences by different barcodes was conducted during the on-instrument base-calling.

RNAeasy kit Qiagen The RNASeq libraries were prepared from total RNA using the TruSeq™ RNA Sample Prep Kit v2 (Illumina Inc., Rev. E, October 2013). Briefly, after poly-A based mRNA enrichment with oligo-dT magnetic beads of at least 0.52 μg of total RNA as input material, the mRNA was fragmented to 80-250 nt, with the major peak at 130 nt. The second strand cDNA synthesis was performed in the presence of dUTP instead of dTTP, to achieve the strand specificity. The blunt-ended double stranded cDNA was 3´adenylated and Illumina indexed adapters were ligated. The ligation product was enriched with 15 PCR cycles and the final library was validated on an Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer with the DNA 7500 assay. The libraries were sequenced on HiSeq2000 (Illumina, Inc) in paired-end mode with a read length of 2x76 bp using the TruSeq SBS Kit v4 (Illumina). Image analysis, base calling and quality scoring of the run were processed using the manufacturer's software Real Time Analysis (RTA 1.18.66.3) and followed by generation of FASTQ sequence files by CASAVA.

The blunt-ended double stranded cDNA was 3´adenylated and Illumina indexed adapters were ligated. The ligation product was enriched with 15 PCR cycles and the final library was validated on an Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer with the DNA 7500 assay. The libraries were sequenced on HiSeq2000 (Illumina, Inc) in paired-end mode with a read length of 2x76 bp using the TruSeq SBS Kit v4 (Illumina). Image analysis, base calling and quality scoring of the run were processed using the manufacturer's software Real Time Analysis (RTA 1.18.66.3) and followed by generation of FASTQ sequence files by CASAVA.

A total of 200 larvae were pooled for each sample, homogenized in quanidinium isothiocyanate buffer and extracted using the hot acid phenol extraction method as outlined by Chomczynski and Sacchi (1987). RNA was extracted over 1:1 acid phenol twice, precipitated over two volumes of 100% ethanol, then resuspended in buffer and treated with Proteinase K to remove remaining protein, re-extracted over 1:1 phenol:chloroform, extracted 1x over chloroform and precipitated over two volumes of ethanol and 0.3M sodium acetate. From this, a total of 30 micrograms of RNA was used for DNase I treatment using the Ambion DNA-Free kit to remove residual DNA. DNA-free RNA was then re-extracted over 1:1 phenol:chloroform followed by final extraction over chloroform, precipitation over two volumes of 100% ethanol and 0.3M sodium acetate. The precipitated pellet was washed two times over 80% ethanol, resuspended in sterile deionized water and sent for sequencing. Paired end libraries were prepared according to Illumina's instructions accompanying the mRNA Seq 8-sample kit by the Hudson Alpha Institute of Biotechnology, Huntsville, AL. Libraries were then sequenced on the Illumina HiSeq 2000 following the manufacturer's protocols. Lengths of 51 nucleotides were sequenced for each of the paired ends and the average insert size was 200 nucleotides.

Insect tissue was crosslinked and then lysed with nuclei permeabilized but still intact. DNA was then restricted and the overhangs filled in incorporating a biotinylated base. Free ends were then ligated together in situ. Crosslinks were reversed, the DNA was sheared to 300-500bp and then biotinylated ligation junctions were recovered with streptavidin beads. Standard Illumina library construction protocol. Briefly, DNA was end-repaired using a combination of T4 DNA polymerase, E. coli DNA Pol I large fragment (Klenow polymerase) and T4 polynucleotide kinase. The blunt, phosphorylated ends were treated with Klenow fragment (32 to 52 exo minus) and dATP to yield a protruding 3- 'A' base for ligation of Illumina's adapters which have a single 'T' base overhang at the 3’ end. After adapter ligation DNA was PCR amplified with Illumina primers for 8-12 cycles and library fragments of 400-600 bp (insert plus adaptor and PCR primer sequences) were purified using SPRI beads. The purified DNA was captured on an Illumina flow cell for cluster generation. Libraries were sequenced on the NextSeq500 following the manufacturer's protocols. In situ Hi-C (Rao, Huntley et al., 2014)

DNA was isolated using a Qiagen Dneasy Mini kit (Qiagen, Valencia, CA) following the manufacturer’s recommendations. Libraries were prepared following the steps provided in Urich et al. (2015; Nature Protocols)

Total RNA of 30 mosquito females of each was extracted using the TRIzol. Total RNA samples were pooled and 10 mg from each pool was used to isolate poly(A) mRNA and to prepare non-directional Illumina RNA-Seq libraries. RNA libraries were prepared for sequencing using standard Illumina protocols

A total of 200 females were pooled for each sample, homogenized in quanidinium isothiocyanate buffer and extracted using the hot acid phenol extraction method as outlined by Chomczynski and Sacchi (1987). RNA was extracted over 1:1 acid phenol twice, precipitated over two volumes of 100% ethanol, then resuspended in buffer and treated with Proteinase K to remove remaining protein, re-extracted over 1:1 phenol:chloroform, extracted 1x over chloroform and precipitated over two volumes of ethanol and 0.3M sodium acetate. From this, a total of 30 micrograms of RNA was used for DNase I treatment using the Ambion DNA-Free kit to remove residual DNA. DNA-free RNA was then re-extracted over 1:1 phenol:chloroform followed by final extraction over chloroform, precipitation over two volumes of 100% ethanol and 0.3M sodium acetate. Paired end libraries were prepared according to Illumina's instructions accompanying the mRNA Seq 8-sample kit by the Hudson Alpha Institute of Biotechnology, Huntsville, AL. Libraries were then sequenced on the Illumina HiSeq 2000 following the manufacturer's protocols. Lengths of 51 nucleotides were sequenced for each of the paired ends and the average insert size was 200 nucleotides.
